page: Amazon Web Services r6id.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The instance provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the r6id.2xlarge consistently in the average category for most compute and memory tasks. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench single-core (1627.0) and multi-core (6073.0) scores, and Passmark CPU score (11254.46) all align with mid-range performance. Memory bandwidth is measured at 413.84 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 59.48 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falls into the weak category at 31.93 GB/sec.

For database operations, the instance achieves an average Passmark database score of 3864.41 and handles 7.76 million Redis SET operations per second. While it handles small LLM inference models at average speeds, performance drops significantly on medium models, with prompt processing falling to a poor 15.19 tokens/sec. This indicates a clear tradeoff: the instance is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and general-purpose data processing, but is inefficient for larger machine learning inference workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services r6id.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Benchmarks show average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 6073.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 11254.46. Memory read bandwidth reaches 413.84 GB/sec for cached small blocks. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference show weaker relative performance, with 7B model prompt processing falling into the bottom 10%. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe storage, while showing clear performance tradeoffs for complex parallel decompression and larger machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering average database and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 474 GB NVMe SSD local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 instance featuring local NVMe SSD storage, balanced for database operations and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
Weak performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference
Suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
